openmeter/notification.purpose: Notification domain root: defines all shared vocabulary (Channel, Rule, Event, EventPayload/RuleConfig/ChannelConfig union types, EventDeliveryStatus) plus the composite Service and EventHandler interfaces. Implementation is split across sub-packages — only types, interfaces, and constants live in the root, so any concrete code here would create import cycles between adapter, service, consumer, and eventhandler.

patterns.name: Root is types+interfaces only; subtrees implement
patterns.description: service.go/event.go/channel.go/rule.go/eventpayload.go/repository.go define contracts; adapter/ (Ent), service/ (orchestration), httpdriver/ (v1 HTTP), consumer/ (Kafka), eventhandler/ (dispatch+reconcile), webhook/ (Svix/noop) each import the root for shared types. Never import a sub-package from the root.
patterns.example: type Service interface { FeatureService; ChannelService; RuleService; EventService }

patterns.name: Every Input declares compile-time Validator assertions
patterns.description: Each *Input type carries `var _ models.Validator` and `var _ models.CustomValidator[T]` and accumulates errors via models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — never raw errors.
patterns.example: func (i CreateRuleInput) Validate() error { /* errs ... */ return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) }

patterns.name: Type-discriminated union Validate()
patterns.description: ChannelConfig, RuleConfig, EventPayload switch on a *Meta.Type discriminator (EventType) and delegate. Adding a new event/channel type requires updating the union struct, its Validate() switch, AND the eventTypes registry in event.go.
patterns.example: switch c.Type { case EventTypeBalanceThreshold: return c.BalanceThreshold.Validate(); ... }

patterns.name: eventTypes slice is the single EventType registry
patterns.description: EventType.Validate() uses lo.Contains(eventTypes, t). A new EventType const must be appended to eventTypes in event.go, not just declared.

patterns.name: EventPayloadVersionCurrent on all new payloads
patterns.description: EventPayloadMeta.Version = EventPayloadVersionCurrent (1) on construction; the adapter branches on version to migrate older invoice payloads.

patterns.name: Cross-cutting limits enforced via named constants
patterns.description: MaxChannelsPerRule (5) is checked inside Create/UpdateRuleInput.Validate(); webhook/ enforces MaxChannelsPerWebhook. Never inline the numeric limit.
patterns.example: if len(i.Channels) > MaxChannelsPerRule { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(...)) }

key_file_guides.role: Composite Service = FeatureService + ChannelService + RuleService + EventService; the dependency surface for all handlers and consumers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods directly to Service instead of a sub-interface breaks smallest-surface dependency.

key_file_guides.role: eventTypes registry, EventType, Event struct, *EventInput types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New EventType const without appending to eventTypes — Validate() rejects it.

key_file_guides.role: EventPayload union + EventPayloadVersionCurrent; Validate() is the payload-correctness entry point.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New payload variant without updating the Validate() switch silently bypasses validation.

key_file_guides.role: EventHandler = EventDispatcher + EventReconciler + Start/Close; default reconcile/dispatch timeouts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dispatch is fire-and-forget — implementations must not block.

key_file_guides.role: Persistence contract (Channel/Rule/EventRepository + entutils.TxCreator) implemented only by adapter/.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Importing the adapter sub-package here causes an import cycle.

key_file_guides.role: Channel/Rule + their *Config union types with discriminated Validate() and ListChannelsInput/ListRulesInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New channel/rule type not handled in the Config.Validate() switch silently passes.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic (Svix calls, Ent queries) to the root package — it is types+interfaces only.
Declaring a new EventType const without appending it to the eventTypes slice in event.go.
Returning raw errors from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(...)) — breaks HTTP 400 mapping via GenericErrorEncoder.
Extending an EventPayload/RuleConfig/ChannelConfig union without updating its Validate() switch.
Importing openmeter/notification/adapter, /service, /consumer, or /webhook/svix from the root — creates import cycles.

decisions.decision: Root holds only types, interfaces, and constants.
decisions.rationale: All sub-packages import the root for shared vocabulary; any implementation here would create cycles between adapter, service, consumer, and eventhandler.

decisions.decision: Single EventPayloadVersionCurrent int rather than per-event versioning.
decisions.rationale: Keeps adapter migration logic simple — it branches on version only for event families whose schema changed (currently invoice).

decisions.decision: EventHandler split into EventDispatcher and EventReconciler.
decisions.rationale: Lets the Watermill consumer depend on the smaller EventDispatcher while the standalone service drives the full reconcile loop.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new event type: extend the registry and union payload

openmeter/sink.purpose: High-throughput Kafka-to-ClickHouse sink worker: consumes raw CloudEvent messages from Kafka partitions, deduplicates (Redis or in-batch), batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, then fires post-flush callbacks so downstream (balance-worker) can recalculate entitlements. Primary constraint: an exactly-once guarantee enforced by strict three-phase flush ordering.

patterns.name: Three-phase flush ordering: storage -> offset commit -> Redis dedupe
patterns.description: flush() always persists to ClickHouse (persistToStorage), then Consumer.StoreMessage for Kafka offsets, then dedupeSet to Redis. Reversing breaks exactly-once on consumer restart. The full sequence runs under s.mu, which also serialises partition pause/resume with Close.
patterns.example: // sink.go: 1. persistToStorage -> 2. Consumer.StoreMessage -> 3. dedupeSet (under s.mu, partitions paused)

patterns.name: FlushEventHandler dispatched in a goroutine, never blocking
patterns.description: OnFlushSuccess runs in a goroutine bounded by FlushSuccessTimeout. Calling it synchronously inside flush() backpressures Kafka partitions. The flushhandler/ FlushEventHandlers multiplexer fans out to downstream handlers with two-phase drain.
patterns.example: go func(){ ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.config.FlushSuccessTimeout); defer cancel(); s.config.FlushEventHandler.OnFlushSuccess(ctx, messages) }()

patterns.name: SinkConfig.Validate() gates construction; Storage injected, not instantiated
patterns.description: NewSink calls config.Validate() with explicit nil/zero checks for every required field before returning. Storage is the injected interface (ClickHouseStorage in prod, mock in tests) — never new ClickHouseStorage inside sink.go.
patterns.example: type Storage interface { BatchInsert(ctx context.Context, messages []sinkmodels.SinkMessage) error }

patterns.name: Meter definitions served from NamespacedMeterCache, never meter.Service in the hot path
patterns.description: meters.go caches namespace->MetersByType behind an RWMutex with a periodic refetch goroutine started by Start(); the consume path calls GetAffectedMeters, never meter.Service directly.

patterns.name: SinkBuffer keyed by TopicPartition+Offset; revoke on rebalance
patterns.description: buffer.go is a mutex-guarded map keyed by KafkaMessage.String() so Add is idempotent within a batch. RemoveByPartitions(revoked) must run before a rebalance completes to drop messages for revoked partitions.

key_file_guides.role: Sink struct, SinkConfig + Validate, NewSink, flush() orchestration, Run() event loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: s.mu must cover the entire ClickHouse+offset+Redis sequence; any reorder risks an exactly-once violation.

key_file_guides.role: Storage interface + ClickHouseStorage; maps SinkMessage to streaming.RawEvent, generates a ULID StoreRowID per event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ingested_at/stored_at fall back to clock.Now(); a parse/insert error fails the whole batch (retry).

key_file_guides.role: SinkBuffer in-batch dedup map; Add idempotent, Dequeue with transformers, RemoveByPartitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Call RemoveByPartitions before rebalance completes to avoid double processing.

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedMeterCache: RWMutex namespace->MetersByType, atomic isRunning, periodic refresh.
key_file_guides.watch_for: isRunning is set before the ticker starts; double-Start is a no-op but the cache won't refresh.

key_file_guides.role: Pure types: SinkMessage pipeline carrier, ProcessingStatus/ProcessingState (OK/DROP).
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic; importing streaming/meter here creates a circular dependency. Use GetDedupeItem() rather than building dedupe.Item by hand.

key_file_guides.role: FlushEventHandler buffered async impl with two-phase shutdown (stopChan then drain) and FlushEventHandlers fan-out mux.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Call WaitForDrain() before process exit or buffered flush notifications are dropped.

anti_patterns: Writing Redis dedupe before the Kafka offset commit — violates exactly-once on restart.
Calling FlushEventHandler.OnFlushSuccess synchronously inside flush() — backpressures Kafka partitions.
Querying meter.Service inside the hot consume path instead of NamespacedMeterCache — per-message DB round trips.
Instantiating ClickHouseStorage inside sink.go instead of injecting via SinkConfig.Storage — breaks testability.
Adding a ProcessingState iota without a String() case and persistToStorage() handling — 'unknown state type' at runtime.

decisions.decision: Flush is protected by a mutex that also covers partition pause/resume.
decisions.rationale: Prevents the sink closing mid-flush during a rebalance and keeps ClickHouse write + offset commit + Redis dedupe atomic from the consumer's view.

decisions.decision: Storage is an injected interface, not instantiated inside Sink.
decisions.rationale: Allows ClickHouse test doubles and aligns with the layered adapter pattern used across the codebase.

decisions.decision: FlushEventHandler runs in a goroutine with FlushSuccessTimeout.
decisions.rationale: Post-flush balance-recalculation notification must not block the ingest path; the timeout bounds the goroutine even if downstream is slow.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new FlushEventHandler for post-flush side effects

openmeter/watermill.purpose: Kafka-backed pub-sub abstraction for all async domain events between OpenMeter binaries. Owns three named topics (ingest, system, balance-worker), event-name-prefix topic routing, the CloudEvents 1.0 wire format, the standard router middleware stack, and kafka/noop driver selection. Every producer and consumer depends on its sub-packages — never on raw Watermill or confluent-kafka-go.

patterns.name: Publish only via eventbus.Publisher — never a topic string
patterns.description: eventbus/ wraps cqrs.EventBus; GeneratePublishTopic routes by EventName() prefix matched against EventVersionSubsystem constants (ingest -> IngestEventsTopic, balance-worker -> BalanceWorkerEventsTopic, default -> SystemEventsTopic). The Publisher interface is the single injection point; a second instance outside app/common Wire risks an inconsistent TopicMapping.

patterns.name: Workers build routers only via router.NewDefaultRouter
patterns.description: router/ wires a fixed ordered middleware stack: PoisonQueue (outermost), DLQ telemetry+OTel, CorrelationID, Recoverer, exponential-backoff Retry, optional ProcessingTimeout preceded by RestoreContext (watermill#467 workaround), HandlerMetrics (innermost). Never construct *message.Router directly.

patterns.name: Unknown ce_types silently dropped in NoPublishingHandler
patterns.description: grouphandler/ dispatches one Kafka message to all GroupEventHandlers matching the CloudEvents ce_type header, with errors.Join fan-out and per-type OTel metrics. Unregistered types are ACKed (dropped), never errored — returning an error retries and DLQ-poisons valid sibling families during rolling deploys.
patterns.example: noPub := grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler(marshaler, grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler(func(ctx, ev *billingevents.InvoiceCreated) error { return svc.OnInvoiceCreated(ctx, ev) }))

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 events implement marshaler.Event
patterns.description: marshaler/ serializes each event to a CloudEvents JSON envelope (ce_type/ce_time/ce_source/ce_subject). EventName() must be stable (drives routing and dispatch); Marshal sets ce_type from it. ULID auto-ID when empty, time.Now() when zero. Validate() runs on both producer and consumer sides; WithSource injects source without mutating the struct.
patterns.example: func (e *MyEvent) EventName() string { return myEventName } // stable, prefix-matched

patterns.name: MaxRetries off-by-one: 0 = one attempt then DLQ
patterns.description: router.Options.MaxRetries=0 means zero retries (one attempt, then DLQ); use 1 for one attempt plus one retry. Never set 0 expecting 'no DLQ'.
patterns.example: // MaxRetries=0 -> 1 attempt -> DLQ; MaxRetries=1 -> 1 attempt + 1 retry -> DLQ

key_file_guides.role: Publisher interface + EventBus; topic routing by event-name prefix; NewMock for tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Event names without a registered subsystem prefix silently route to SystemEventsTopic; nil Publisher in Options errors in New().

key_file_guides.role: NoPublishingHandler ce_type multiplexer; AddHandler is RWMutex-guarded and safe post-construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never error on unknown event types; don't mutate a shared deserialized event pointer when handlers share a type.

key_file_guides.role: CloudEvents 1.0 serializer/deserializer; calls Validate() on deserialize; WithSource wrapper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Too-strict Validate() rejects valid older-producer events during rolling deploys; never set CloudEventsHeaderType manually.
Add Timeout only with RestoreContext immediately before it; missing Tracer/MetricMeter nil-panics HandlerMetrics.

key_file_guides.role: Organizational parent for kafka/ (IBM Sarama, prod) and noop/ (disabled-feature Wire paths). Selection in app/common only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No .go files belong directly under driver/; a new driver needs a noop counterpart.

key_file_guides.role: Adapters between NoPublishHandlerFunc and HandlerFunc signatures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler fails with ErrMessagesProduced if the adapted handler ever produces — use only for pure consumers.

anti_patterns: Constructing a *message.Router directly instead of router.NewDefaultRouter — loses DLQ, OTel, and Retry middleware.
Defining an EventName() without a stable EventVersionSubsystem prefix — silently routes to SystemEventsTopic.
Adding source files directly under openmeter/watermill/driver/ — code belongs in kafka/ or noop/.
Setting MaxRetries=0 expecting no DLQ — that is one attempt then DLQ.
Publishing to a raw Kafka topic string from domain code — bypasses eventbus routing and consumer-group isolation.

decisions.decision: Three fixed topics with prefix-based routing inside eventbus.
decisions.rationale: Topic isolation matches the ingest/balance/system worker topology; centralising routing stops producers writing to the wrong consumer group.

decisions.decision: CloudEvents 1.0 as the universal wire format.
decisions.rationale: Standard ce_type-header routing for grouphandler and interoperability with Svix webhook payloads.

decisions.decision: Unknown event types are dropped, not errored, in NoPublishingHandler.
decisions.rationale: Producers and consumers deploy independently — a consumer can ignore types it has not yet registered without poisoning its DLQ.

code_examples.scenario: Registering a new event handler in a worker's NoPublishingHandler
